text: The 6th of February 2023, is recorded in the history of our country as one of the most devastating earthquakes. The fact that 10 provinces were affected to a large extent increased the damage. The Ministry of National Education (MoNE) started to provide education and services for humanitarian aid to the affected population. With its human resources and production capacity, the Ministry has provided significant services in terms of reducing the effects of the earthquake. This study aims to evaluate the activities carried out by the MoNE in the context of the earthquake occurring in our country within the framework of international literature, and to examine the activities implemented by the Ministry within the scope of Education in Emergencies (EiE) standards determined by INEE and UNESCO-IIEP in order to provide continuity of the continuity of education activities, and the social aid activities implemented by the Ministry within the scope of the epidemic experienced during the Covid-19 period. The implementations carried out by MoNE institutions in the regions where the earthquakes occurred have contributed positively to the humanitarian aid activities in the region. MoNE has undertaken the majority of the production and delivery of food, accommodation and heating materials for the affected population. In addition, the Ministry has rapidly set up education areas in the affected regions and appointed teachers and counselors to these areas in order to implement education activities. While temporary education areas were implemented in tents, containers and prefabricated schools in the earthquake-affected regions to ensure the continuity of education activities as soon as possible, the necessary arrangements have also been made for face-to-face education activities. Hospital classes have been established in hospitals so that students under treatment can continue their education. The re-location of students to other cities for education has been made easy and free boarding arrangements have been provided for them.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
